claim: Decomposes the first-difference quotient Q_w on the ordered branch x_0>=x_1 by substituting x_0=x_1+c. Nonnegative cone groups are certified positive; residual groups are isolated.
claim_boundary: This is a reduction certificate, not a final proof of barrier positivity of Q_w.

theorem.name: Ordered-branch Q cone decomposition
theorem.proof_use: This is the next reduction toward proving Q_w>=0 on the barrier: the residual terms are the only terms not already nonnegative on the ordered branch.
theorem.remaining_target: Prove residual_Q_w>=0 under the barrier condition, or absorb it into a barrier multiple plus an ordered-cone-positive certificate.
theorem.result: The decomposition separates Q_w into an ordered-cone-positive part and a residual part that still needs the barrier condition.
theorem.statement: On the ordered branch x_0>=x_1, substitute x_0=x_1+c with c>=0. Terms with nonnegative coefficients in (c,x_1,rest) are automatically certified positive.
theorem.why_this_is_not_final: Some rest-exponent groups remain residual with negative coefficients after x_0=x_1+c; those terms encode the real barrier obstruction.
